title: Pit Window Distance-to-Optimum Features
summary: Strategy models approximate optimum stint length from pit loss and degradation rate; public F1 strategy guides describe the stop decision as fresh-tyre gain versus pit-loss cost.
rationale: Strategy models approximate optimum stint length from pit loss and degradation rate; public F1 strategy guides describe the stop decision as fresh-tyre gain versus pit-loss cost. Current solutions include tyre-life medians, but not an explicit distance from expected compound/race/stint pit window.
implementation_hint: Using train only for supervised-free aggregates, estimate median and quantiles of observed TyreLife/LapNumber at PitNextLap=1 by Race, Year, Compound, Stint, and fallback Compound-Stint. Add distance_to_pit_window, inside_25_75_window, past_p90_window, and normalized tyre age versus expected stop age to train/test.
expected_effect: Can add a strong prior for when each compound/stint typically stops while allowing GBM/XGB to adjust for live degradation and position context.
risk: Aggregating only positive stops can overfit rare race-compound groups; use smoothed fallbacks and avoid including the current row's target in out-of-fold train encodings if implemented as target-derived statistics.
